Half: $\tfrac{1}{2}\pi r^2 h$ - Quarter - Sector: $\tfrac{\theta}{360}\pi r^2 h$ ::: ::::: ::::: lt-item ::: lt-number 4 ::: ::: lt-content **Volume — Reverse** Rearrange volume formulas to find unknown dimensions such as length, height, or radius. ::: ::::: ::::: lt-item ::: lt-number 5 ::: ::: lt-content **Volume — Context** Solve real-world problems involving volume, including converting m³ to litres. ::: ::::: ::::: lt-item ::: lt-number 6 ::: ::: lt-content **Composite Volume** Break complex solids into simpler shapes and add or subtract their volumes. ::: ::::: :::::::::::::::::: --- ### 🟢 Surface Area :::::::::::::::::: lt-card ::::: lt-item ::: lt-number 7 ::: ::: lt-content **SA — Prisms** Calculate surface area of cuboids, triangular and trapezoidal prisms, and open boxes. ::: ::::: ::::: lt-item ::: lt-number 8 ::: ::: lt-content **SA — Cylinders** Calculate surface area of: - Closed cylinders - Open-top cylinders - Tubes and half-cylinders ::: ::::: ::::: lt-item ::: lt-number 9 ::: ::: lt-content **SA — Composite** Find surface area of composite solids, including added or removed faces. ::: ::::: :::::::::::::::::: --- ### 🟣 Advanced & Algebra :::::::::::::::::: lt-card ::::: lt-item ::: lt-number 10 ::: ::: lt-content **Unit Conversions** Convert between length, area, and volume units using correct powers of conversion. ::: ::::: ::::: lt-item ::: lt-number 11 ::: ::: lt-content **Pyramids, Cones & Spheres** - Pyramid: $V=\tfrac{1}{3}Ah$ - Cone: $V=\tfrac{1}{3}\pi r^2h$, $SA=\pi r^2+\pi rl$ - Sphere: $V=\tfrac{4}{3}\pi r^3$, $SA=4\pi r^2$ Includes composite solids. ::: ::::: ::::: lt-item ::: lt-number 12 ::: ::: lt-content **Algebraic Volume** Work with algebraic dimensions: - Expand and simplify expressions - Solve for $x$ given volume ::: ::::: :::::::::::::::::: --- ## 🗂️ Formula Cards ::::::::::::::: formula-card ### Unit 3: Mensuration — Formula Reference {.unnumbered} --- #### 📏 Unit Conversions {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-yellow **💡 Key Rule** When converting units:\ • **Length** → multiply or divide by the factor\ • **Area** → use the factor **squared**\ • **Volume** → use the factor **cubed** ::: ::: rule-group **Length chain:** $\text{km} \xrightarrow{\times 1000} \text{m} \xrightarrow{\times 100} \text{cm} \xrightarrow{\times 10} \text{mm}$ **Area chain:** $\text{km}^2 \xrightarrow{\times 10^6} \text{m}^2 \xrightarrow{\times 10^4} \text{cm}^2 \xrightarrow{\times 100} \text{mm}^2$ **Volume chain:** $\text{km}^3 \xrightarrow{\times 10^9} \text{m}^3 \xrightarrow{\times 10^6} \text{cm}^3 \xrightarrow{\times 10^3} \text{mm}^3$ **Capacity:** $1\text{ m}^3 = 1000\text{ L}$ $\qquad$ $1\text{ cm}^3 = 1\text{ mL}$ ::: ::: sticky-note-pink **⚠️ Common Error** $1\text{ m}^2 = 100\text{ cm}^2$ ✗ — should be **10 000** cm².\ $1\text{ m}^3 = 100\text{ cm}^3$ ✗ — should be **1 000 000** cm³. ::: --- #### 📦 Volume Formulae {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-blue **🎯 Core Idea** For ANY prism or cylinder:\ $$V = A_{\text{cross-section}} \times \text{length}$$ ::: ::: rule-group | Solid | Cross-section | Formula | |-------|-------------|---------| | Rectangular prism | Rectangle | $V = l \times w \times h$ | | Triangular prism | Triangle | $V = \tfrac{1}{2}\,b\,h\,l$ | | Trapezoidal prism | Trapezoid | $V = \tfrac{1}{2}(a+b)\,h\,l$ | | Cylinder | Circle | $V = \pi r^2 h$ | | Half-cylinder | Semicircle | $V = \tfrac{1}{2}\pi r^2 h$ | | Quarter-cylinder | Quarter circle | $V = \tfrac{1}{4}\pi r^2 h$ | | Cylindrical sector | Sector ($\theta°$) | $V = \dfrac{\theta}{360}\,\pi r^2 h$ | **Composite solids:** $V_{\text{total}} = V_1 + V_2$ (add) or $V_1 - V_2$ (subtract for holes) ::: ::: sticky-note-pink **⚠️ Common Errors**\ - Using diameter instead of radius: $r = d \div 2$ first!\ - Missing the $\tfrac{1}{2}$ for triangular cross-sections.\ - Adding volumes when you should subtract (hollow solids). ::: --- #### 🎨 Surface Area Formulae {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-orange **📝 Strategy** Surface Area = **sum of areas of ALL outer faces**.\ Sketch the solid, label every face, calculate each, then add. ::: ::: rule-group **Cuboid (all 6 faces):** $$SA = 2(lw + lh + wh)$$ **Open-top box (5 faces — no lid):** $$SA = lw + 2lh + 2wh$$ **Triangular prism (5 faces):** $$SA = 2 \times A_{\triangle} + (b + s_1 + s_2) \times l$$ **Trapezoidal prism (6 faces):** $$SA = 2 \times \tfrac{1}{2}(a+b)h + (a + b + s_1 + s_2) \times l$$ **Closed cylinder (curved + 2 circles):** $$SA = 2\pi rh + 2\pi r^2$$ **Open-top cylinder (curved + 1 circle):** $$SA = 2\pi rh + \pi r^2$$ **Tube/pipe (curved only, both ends open):** $$SA = 2\pi rh$$ **Half-cylinder (3 regions):** $$SA = \underbrace{\pi r h}_{\text{half-curved}} + \underbrace{2rh}_{\text{flat rect}} + \underbrace{\pi r^2}_{\text{2 semicircles}}$$ ::: ::: sticky-note-yellow **💡 Cylinder Unrolled** The curved surface of a cylinder unrolls into a rectangle:\ width $= 2\pi r$ (the circumference), height $= h$.\ So curved $SA = 2\pi r \times h = 2\pi rh$. ::: ::: sticky-note-pink **⚠️ Common Errors — Surface Area**\ - Using the full cylinder SA formula when the cylinder is open at one or both ends.\ - Forgetting to include **both** triangular ends of a triangular prism.\ - Counting an internal join face that is **not** on the outside surface.\ - Confusing SA (cm²) with Volume (cm³). ::: --- #### 🔺 Pyramids, Cones and Spheres {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-blue **🎯 Key Pattern** Pyramids and cones are exactly **⅓** the volume of the prism or cylinder with the same base and height. ::: ::: rule-group | Solid | Volume | Surface Area | |-------|--------|-------------| | Pyramid (any base) | $V = \dfrac{1}{3} A_{\text{base}} \times h$ | sum of all faces | | Rectangular pyramid | $V = \dfrac{1}{3} lwh$ | | | Square pyramid | $V = \dfrac{1}{3} s^2 h$ | | | Cone | $V = \dfrac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$ | $SA = \pi r^2 + \pi r l$ | | Sphere | $V = \dfrac{4}{3}\pi r^3$ | $SA = 4\pi r^2$ | | Hemisphere | $V = \dfrac{2}{3}\pi r^3$ | $SA = 3\pi r^2$ | **Cone slant height:** $l = \sqrt{r^2 + h^2}$ (Pythagoras — slant is the hypotenuse)\ **Remember:** $r = d \div 2$ always before substituting. ::: ::: sticky-note-pink **⚠️ Common Errors — Curved Solids**\ - Forgetting the $\tfrac{1}{3}$ in pyramid and cone formulas.\ - Squaring $r$ when you should cube it (sphere formula uses $r^3$).\ - Using $\tfrac{4}{3}$ instead of $\tfrac{2}{3}$ for hemisphere volume.\ - Using the perpendicular height $h$ instead of slant $l$ in cone SA. ::: --- #### 🔣 Algebraic Volume {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-orange **📝 Method** Substitute the algebraic expressions into the volume formula, then expand brackets and collect like terms using index laws. ::: ::: rule-group **Steps:** 1. Write the formula: e.g. $V = \tfrac{1}{2} \times \text{base} \times h \times l$ 2. Substitute each expression: e.g. base $= 2x$, $h = x$, $l = (x+4)$ 3. Multiply step by step: $V = \tfrac{1}{2} \times 2x \times x \times (x+4) = x^2(x+4)$ 4. Expand: $V = x^3 + 4x^2$ **Index law reminder:** $(ax)^2 = a^2x^2$ — square both the coefficient AND the variable. **Example — solve for x:** If $V = 3x^3 = 375$, then $x^3 = 125$, so $x = \sqrt[3]{125} = 5$. ::: ::: sticky-note-pink **⚠️ Common Errors — Algebraic Volume**\ - $(2x)^2 = 4x^2$ ✓ not $2x^2$ ✗\ - Stopping before expanding all brackets.\ - Forgetting $V =$ on the left-hand side of the formula.\ - Square rooting when the equation requires a **cube root**. ::: ::::::::::::::::: --- ## 📏 Part A — Unit Conversions {#sec-conversions} ::: {.callout-note icon=false} ## 🔑 Key Conversion Facts | Dimension | Conversion | |-----------|-----------| | **Length** | $\times10$: cm→mm · $\times100$: m→cm · $\times1000$: km→m | | **Area** | $\times100$: cm²→mm² · $\times10{,}000$: m²→cm² · $\times10^6$: km²→m² | | **Volume** | $\times1000$: cm³→mm³ · $\times10^6$: m³→cm³ · $1\text{ m}^3=1000\text{ L}$ | *To go the other way: divide by the same factor.* ::: :::::::::: panel-tabset ### 🟢 Easy ::::: {#exr-conv-1} ### Converting Units of Length Convert the measurement. a) $5.1\,\text{cm} = \square\,\text{mm}$ b) $3.2\,\text{cm} = \square\,\text{mm}$ c) $4373\,\text{mm} = \square\,\text{m}$ d) $6.7\,\text{km} = \square\,\text{m}$ e) $1.3\,\text{m} = \square\,\text{cm}$ f) $2342\,\text{mm} = \square\,\text{m}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $51\,\text{mm}$ b) $32\,\text{mm}$ c) $4.373\,\text{m}$ d) $6700\,\text{m}$ e) $130\,\text{cm}$ f) $2.342\,\text{m}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
